source.episodeTitle: The One After Ross Says Rachel

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One After Ross Says Rachel

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E01 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: She's in hiding. She's utterly humiliated. She doesn't want to see you ever again.
sourceContext.targetLine: We're very sad that it didn't work out between you and Emily, monkey.
sourceContext.nextLine: But I think you're absolutely delicious.

zh-CN.corePhrase: work out between A and B
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我们都很难过，你和 Emily 最后还是没成，宝贝。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Ross 在婚礼上喊错名字后，家人只能用 work out 这种委婉说法来概括婚姻泡汤。英语里谈感情破裂时常故意说轻一点，既体面，也是在安抚当事人。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 常说 it didn't work out 或 things worked out。谈关系时可接 between us；谈计划和安排时也能表示“结果顺利/不顺利”。

sourceContext.previousLine: I mean, there's no way I can make myself taller now.
sourceContext.targetLine: And who knows what science will come up with in the future...
sourceContext.nextLine: ...but, Chandler, what if I die an unsuccessful, regular-size man?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 谁知道未来科学会想出什么办法呢……
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Chandler 拿未来科技开玩笑，假装安慰 Ross 还有“长高”的希望。这里的 come up with 不是随便想到，而是“研发出一个解决办法”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 最常见搭配是 come up with an idea / a plan / a solution。主语通常是人、团队、science 这类“能产出东西”的对象。

sourceContext.previousLine: What, am I like a complete idiot...
sourceContext.targetLine: ...for thinking she'd actually show up?
sourceContext.nextLine: No.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我竟然还以为她真的会来，是不是蠢透了？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Ross 被当众放鸽子后，show up 直接戳中他的尴尬点: Emily 连到场都不愿意。这个词在约会、婚礼、聚会语境里都很高频。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 比 arrive 更口语，也常带“终于出现”或“居然来了”的情绪。放别人鸽子时常说 didn't show up。

sourceContext.previousLine: Uh, shall I go on?
sourceContext.targetLine: He said "Rachel," right?
sourceContext.nextLine: Do you think I should go up there?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 他说的是“Rachel”，对吧？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Monica 需要别人再次确认那个灾难瞬间是否真的发生过。这里的 right? 不是考知识点，而是情绪化地求确认。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 句尾 right? 很常用来确认对方和自己站在同一认知上，语气比 isn't it? 更口语，也更快。

sourceContext.previousLine: 1 Friends, family, we're gathered to celebrate here today...
sourceContext.targetLine: ...the joyous union of Ross and Emily.
sourceContext.nextLine: Now Ross, repeat after me.

zh-CN.corePhrase: the joyous union of A and B
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: ……来见证 Ross 和 Emily 这场喜事。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这是婚礼司仪那种正式、庄重、偏仪式化的说法，和后面 Ross 失口喊错名字形成强烈反差。认识这种礼仪词，能更好听懂婚礼场景里的正式英语。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 多见于婚礼誓词、主持词或故意模仿正式腔的玩笑里，日常聊天几乎不会这么说。

sourceContext.previousLine: - Hey. - Hey.
sourceContext.targetLine: Wow, I hope you don't take this the wrong way...
sourceContext.nextLine: ...but I know we had plans to meet up tonight and...

definitions: 误会我的意思；往坏处理解
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我真心希望你别误会我的意思……
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 说话人先铺垫一句，表示接下来要说的话可能会伤人或冒犯。美式社交里这是很常见的“先打预防针”句型。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 常用在批评、拒绝、提醒前。别直译成“走错方向”，它本质上是在说“你别把我这话理解偏了”。

sourceContext.previousLine: Ross said my name up there. I mean, come on.
sourceContext.targetLine: I can't just pretend that didn't happen.
sourceContext.nextLine: I don't know.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我没法就这么假装刚才那事没发生过。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 婚礼事故太大，Ross 不可能靠装没事把场面糊过去。这个结构特别适合处理尴尬、越界、失言后的现实反应。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 口语里很常说 let's pretend that never happened，语气通常带尴尬、自嘲，或者明确表示事情已经过线了。

sourceContext.previousLine: ...we still get cake, right?
sourceContext.targetLine: That's all right. No, no. You take your time, sweetie.
sourceContext.nextLine: I'll be out here. Ha, ha.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 没关系，你慢慢来，亲爱的。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句话用在高压婚礼残局里，是典型的安抚型表达。表面上在给时间，实际上也在给对方台阶和情绪缓冲区。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 非常高频，适合服务场景、朋友安抚、等人做决定。语气通常是体贴的，不催人。

source.episodeTitle: The One With All The Kissing

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One With All The Kissing

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E02 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: It's dangerous. She won't get what she wants.
sourceContext.targetLine: And who knows who she might end up hurting?
sourceContext.nextLine: Monica's right. Swing dancing can be tricky.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 谁知道她最后还会伤到谁。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 朋友们在谈 Ross 和 Emily 的后续伤害范围，end up 带的是“事情最后会发展到哪一步”的不确定感。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 高频搭配有 end up alone / end up doing sth / end up with sth。它强调结果，不强调过程。

sourceContext.previousLine: You look cute in bubbles.
sourceContext.targetLine: Ahh, you're just all liquored up.
sourceContext.nextLine: Hey, it's me. I'm coming in.

definitions: 喝得上头的；酒劲正大的
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你这就是喝上头了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这里不是正式地说 drunk，而是更随口、更带调侃地说对方被酒精推着走。Friends 这种熟人互损场景里很常见。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 比 drunk 更口语，更像朋友之间打趣。多用于评价某人言行是“酒劲儿上来”的结果。

sourceContext.previousLine: Later!
sourceContext.targetLine: Shouldn't Rachel be back?
sourceContext.nextLine: Her plane got delayed.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Rachel 不是该回来了吗？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这种 should be back 是很生活化的时间预期表达，默认对方出去一会儿后按理该回来了。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 比 return 口语得多。常见于家人、室友、同事之间催进度或确认人什么时候回来。

term.text: not want to talk about it

sourceContext.previousLine: So he had a really funny hat.
sourceContext.targetLine: I don't want to talk about it.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Hey. - Hey, Ross!

zh-CN.corePhrase: I don't want to talk about it
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我不想谈这个。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Ross 明显还在情绪里，这句就是设边界。比直接爆炸更克制，但也很明确地告诉别人别继续追问。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 这是非常实用的拒谈句型。语气可以平静，也可以很冷，关键看重音落在 don't 还是 talk about it。

sourceContext.previousLine: So, what, that's it?
sourceContext.targetLine: Well, yeah. We're cool. Totally cool.
sourceContext.nextLine: Thanks, Rach. You're the best.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 对啊，我们没事，完全没事。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Ross 嘴上说 we're cool，其实是在硬撑体面。美式社交里 cool 很常用来标记关系是否还顺、是否还有尴尬。
zh-CN.usageNotes: We're cool. 常用于确认关系没有冲突了。语气自然时是和解；说得太用力时反而像在自我安慰。

sourceContext.previousLine: I did not "abandon" Rachel. Okay?
sourceContext.targetLine: Emily showed up at the airport.
sourceContext.nextLine: I had to go after her. I did what I had to do.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Emily 直接跑到机场来了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 在爱情戏里，show up at the airport 是很典型的“最后一刻冲过去”桥段，听懂这个动作词组，剧情张力一下就明白了。
zh-CN.usageNotes: show up 后面常接地点。它天然带一点“不是原计划、但人突然出现了”的戏剧感。

sourceContext.previousLine: Not since I lost her at the airport.
sourceContext.targetLine: I can't believe she can outrun you.
sourceContext.nextLine: Hey! She's fast, okay?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我真不敢信她居然能跑得过你。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这里拿 Ross 被甩还追不上人开玩笑，outrun 把画面感一下拉出来了。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 既能字面表示“跑赢”，也能引申为 outrun the problem 这种“摆脱/甩开”问题的说法。

source.episodeTitle: The One Hundredth

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One Hundredth

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E03 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: What are you talking about?
sourceContext.targetLine: You've been telling me to get over Ross and move on.
sourceContext.nextLine: I'm moving on and so are you.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你一直叫我别再惦记 Ross 了，赶紧往前走。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 临产前还在被感情话题刺激，move on 是朋友劝人别卡在旧关系里的高频表达。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 最常见于 breakup 后的安慰。比 get over 更强调“继续生活”，不一定代表已经完全不难受了。

sourceContext.previousLine: Just relax, everything looks great.
sourceContext.targetLine: And also, I love Fonzie.
sourceContext.nextLine: Did he just say he loves Fonzie?

definitions: Fonzie，《欢乐时光》里那种酷酷的老派电视偶像
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 还有，我超爱 Fonzie。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 疼到语无伦次时突然冒出一个美国电视文化梗。中国学习者就算听清这个词，也未必知道这是在强化她“胡言乱语”的喜感。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 这种专有文化梗不是为了日常复用，而是为了补剧情理解。碰到美国老剧常会出现类似流行文化代号。

sourceContext.previousLine: Hi, kids! Um, it's me, Aunt Phoebe.
sourceContext.targetLine: Can't wait to see you. Please don't hurt me.
sourceContext.nextLine: What took you so long?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 等不及见你了，求你别弄疼我。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 在临产恐惧里提前对医生或护士“喊话”，这种直白的 hurt 在医疗场景里非常常见，不只是情感上的 hurt。
zh-CN.usageNotes: hurt 在口语里既能说身体疼，也能说心里受伤。Please don't hurt me 在医护和玩笑场景里都常见，但语气差别很大。

sourceContext.previousLine: He's head of the department.
sourceContext.targetLine: All right. Ooh. Oh, dear God, save me.
sourceContext.nextLine: I'm having my first contraction.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 天啊，救救我。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 分娩痛到顶点时，Phoebe 直接喊 save me，是一种夸张但完全自然的求救反应。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 口语里既能真的求救，也能夸张地表达“我快撑不住了”。朋友抱怨工作、尴尬、社交灾难时也会这么说。

sourceContext.previousLine: Oh, man! I did it again!
sourceContext.targetLine: Someone has to call Frank and Alice.
sourceContext.nextLine: My mom might... What are you doing?

definitions: 赶紧通知 Frank 和 Alice
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 得有人赶快给 Frank 和 Alice 打电话。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句的重点不是 call 本身，而是临场分工: 一出大事，第一反应就是谁去通知关键人。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 日常里 someone has to call... 很高频，用来在紧急或混乱时迅速分配任务。

sourceContext.previousLine: Okay.
sourceContext.targetLine: I'll miss watching you freak people out like that.
sourceContext.nextLine: Pheebs...

definitions: 把别人吓一跳；把人整不会了
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我会想念你那样把别人吓得一愣一愣的。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 一贯古怪，大家拿她“吓到别人”的能力开玩笑。这个表达特别适合 Friends 这种熟人群像里的损友语气。
zh-CN.usageNotes: freak someone out 指让别人紧张、毛骨悚然或不自在。既可真吓到，也可说某人太怪了让人发毛。

source.episodeTitle: The One Where Phoebe Hates PBS

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One Where Phoebe Hates PBS

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E04 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: She's talking.
sourceContext.targetLine: When you said "the deal with Santa Claus," you meant?
sourceContext.nextLine: That he doesn't exist.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你说“圣诞老人那档子事”，你到底指什么？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 追问对方真实意思时，用 the deal with 把抽象吐槽具体化。这个结构在美剧里非常高频。
zh-CN.usageNotes: What's the deal with...? 常用来追问某人、某事的来龙去脉，也常出现在吐槽和脱口秀语境。

sourceContext.previousLine: That's great!
sourceContext.targetLine: A little good deed for PBS, plus some TV exposure.
sourceContext.nextLine: That's the kind of math Joey likes to do.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 给 PBS 做件好事，还能顺便混点电视曝光。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Joey 一边装公益，一边惦记露脸机会。娱乐圈里 exposure 不等于真正的报酬，但能带来可见度。
zh-CN.usageNotes: exposure 常用于媒体、娱乐、时尚行业，指“被看见的机会”。可正面，也可带点“不给钱只给曝光”的讽刺意味。

sourceContext.previousLine: ...hoping someone will help him get in touch with her.
sourceContext.targetLine: I don't care if I said some other girl's name, you prissy old twit!
sourceContext.nextLine: Ross.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我才不管我是不是喊了别的女孩名字，你这个矫情的老女人！
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Ross 和 Emily 通话彻底失控，这句是情绪爆炸时的伤人话。它有社交风险，但能帮助学习者听懂争吵戏里那种轻蔑口吻。
zh-CN.usageNotes: twit 是偏英式、带轻蔑感的骂法；prissy 则强调对方过分讲究、娇气、爱端着。不要在真实关系里乱用。

sourceContext.previousLine: I had to talk loud because the movie was loud!
sourceContext.targetLine: - He's talking to London. - Did he get in touch with Emily?
sourceContext.nextLine: No. He's calling everyone on her side of the family...

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 他联系上 Emily 了吗？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Ross 和 Emily 冷战期里，能不能联系上本人就是剧情关键。这个短语在电话、邮件、社交软件时代都一直高频。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 比 contact 更口语。常说 get in touch with me / try to get in touch with her。

sourceContext.previousLine: Aha. That one!
sourceContext.targetLine: Give me a second. I'll be out of your hair. I'm just going to grab a jacket.
sourceContext.nextLine: When I get back, I want every little detail.

definitions: 不再烦你；马上从你眼前消失
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 给我一秒，我拿件夹克就不烦你了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 说话人知道自己在打扰别人，所以先用 out of your hair 降低冒犯感。这是带点讨好意味的美式客套。
zh-CN.usageNotes: I'll get out of your hair 很常见，意思不是“离你的头发远点”，而是“我不占你时间、不继续烦你了”。

sourceContext.previousLine: Well, here's your phone, doctor.
sourceContext.targetLine: I can't believe I can't find a selfless good deed.
sourceContext.nextLine: You know that really old guy that lives next door to me?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我真不敢信，我居然找不到一件完全无私的好事可做。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 想证明世上存在“纯无私”的善行，这正是本集的道德笑点。认识 good deed 这类词，能更快抓住她的执念。
zh-CN.usageNotes: good deed 是偏口语又很常见的“善举”。加 selfless 后强调动机纯粹，不掺回报。

sourceContext.previousLine: That's great!
sourceContext.targetLine: A little good deed for PBS, plus some TV exposure.
sourceContext.nextLine: That's the kind of math Joey likes to do.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 给 PBS 做件好事，再顺带有点电视曝光。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 同一句里把 good deed 和 TV exposure 放一起，本身就在讽刺“做好事”被夹带私货。
zh-CN.usageNotes: do a good deed 很常见，语气比 charitable act 轻松得多，也更像日常口语。

source.episodeTitle: The One With The Kips

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One With The Kips

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E05 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: I'm going to the bathroom.
sourceContext.targetLine: Man, if anyone asked me to give up any of you, I don't think I could do it.
sourceContext.nextLine: Me neither.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 如果有人要我为了谁放弃你们中的任何一个，我觉得我都做不到。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Monica 把友情和恋爱放在一起比较时，give up 直接把“割舍”说得很重。这个词在关系戏里比 quit 更有情感分量。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 既能说 give up smoking，也能说 give up on a person。前者是放弃做某事，后者更像“对某人不抱希望了”。

sourceContext.previousLine: No, no. It was a fight.
sourceContext.targetLine: You deal with it and you move on. It's nothing to freak out about.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Really? Okay, great. - Heh.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你就面对它，处理掉，然后继续往前走。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是典型的 Friends 式直给安慰: 不绕弯，直接叫人接受现实。英语母语者在熟人之间很爱用这种短而硬的鼓励。
zh-CN.usageNotes: deal with it 语气能从安慰到不耐烦，跨度很大。关键看语调，冷着说会很像“你自己受着吧”。

sourceContext.previousLine: No, no. It was a fight.
sourceContext.targetLine: You deal with it and you move on. It's nothing to freak out about.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Really? Okay, great. - Heh.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 这没什么好大惊小怪的。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 朋友在感情秘密被撞见后，先劝对方别 freak out，意思不是别有情绪，而是别把事态升级得更难收拾。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 既能说 I freaked out，也能说 It freaked me out。前者是自己慌了，后者是被某事吓到了。

sourceContext.previousLine: No, no. It was a fight.
sourceContext.targetLine: You deal with it and you move on. It's nothing to freak out about.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Really? Okay, great. - Heh.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你处理掉，然后继续往前走。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句和 deal with it 连着出现，很像朋友间那种实用主义式情绪处理法: 接受现实，然后别一直困在原地。
zh-CN.usageNotes: move on from someone / from something 很高频。它比 get over 更强调“开始下一阶段”。

sourceContext.previousLine: Condoms?
sourceContext.targetLine: Hey, you don't know how long we're gonna be in here.
sourceContext.nextLine: We may have to re-populate the Earth.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你又不知道我们得在这儿待多久。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Monica 和 Chandler 藏在房间里时，这句体现的是“封闭空间里被迫久待”的现实焦躁。
zh-CN.usageNotes: in here / out there 这类小词组在口语里特别高频。be in here 带的是人在某个封闭或当前空间里待着。

sourceContext.previousLine: I'm really getting tired of sneaking around all the time.
sourceContext.targetLine: I know, me too. Hey, what if we went away for the whole weekend? No interruptions.
sourceContext.nextLine: And we could be naked the entire time.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 要不我们干脆整个周末都出去吧？不会有人打扰。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Monica 提议彻底逃离日常视线，the whole weekend 让这件事一下从“约出去”升级成“短途二人世界”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: whole 常用来强化完整时段: the whole day, the whole week, the whole weekend。

sourceContext.previousLine: ...and we were all stuck in here all night with no food?
sourceContext.targetLine: Well, when Ross said "Rachel" at the wedding, I figured it was gonna happen again.
sourceContext.nextLine: You know? So I hid this in here.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Ross 在婚礼上喊出“Rachel”那一刻，我就知道这事迟早还会再来一次。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 把两人的感情走向说成“早晚会重演”，这里的 happen again 带的是八卦圈对旧情复燃的判断。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 口语里 gonna happen again 很常见，用来判断某种模式、冲突或事故会不会重演。

sourceContext.previousLine: - Hey. - Hey.
sourceContext.targetLine: - Is Rachel here? I gotta talk to her. - No, she's out shopping.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Damn. - What's going on?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Rachel 在吗？我得跟她谈谈。她出去逛街了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这类 be out shopping 非常生活化，立刻能听出人不在家，而且是去做哪种活动了。
zh-CN.usageNotes: be out shopping / be out eating / be out with Gary 都是固定口语套路，表示“人在外面做某事”。

source.episodeTitle: The One With The Yeti

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One With The Yeti

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E06 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: Emily thinks Ross' furniture has got Rachel cooties?
sourceContext.targetLine: - Calm down, Joey. - No.
sourceContext.nextLine: Everything's getting all messed up, you know?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 冷静点，Joey。不要。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 当 Joey 因为秘密关系被曝光而炸毛时，别人第一反应就是让他 calm down。它是高频安抚词，但也可能让对方更火。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 很常见，但在冲突里未必有效。对方已经很激动时，直接说 calm down 反而可能被听成居高临下。

sourceContext.previousLine: I think he's right.
sourceContext.targetLine: You guys hang out at the coffeehouse way too much.
sourceContext.nextLine: - God, I feel so guilty about Ross. - Ugh, I know.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你们也太常泡在咖啡馆了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Emily 对 Central Perk 这种朋友据点不适应，hang out 在这里强调的是“没有明确目的地长期待着”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: hang out at the coffeehouse / with friends 都很自然。语气轻松，不等于正式邀约。

sourceContext.previousLine: What?
sourceContext.targetLine: Look, just because I know about you two doesn't mean I like looking at it.
sourceContext.nextLine: Aren't you supposed to be at an audition for another hour?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我知道你们俩的事，不代表我就想看你们这样。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Joey 夹在秘密情侣之间时，用 about you two 这种说法把“你们的关系”说得既明确又带点嫌弃。
zh-CN.usageNotes: about you two / about you guys 在八卦和关系戏里很高频，用来指两个人之间那段事。

sourceContext.previousLine: 1 Hey, hey, hey, hey.
sourceContext.targetLine: None of that. Not while you're living under my roof.
sourceContext.nextLine: What?

definitions: 在我家里；在我的地盘上
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 不许那样，尤其是你还住在我家屋檐下的时候。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Monica 用 under my roof 把普通抱怨升级成“这是我家、我定规矩”的家长式口气。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 很适合表达居住空间带来的管理权。语气通常偏强势，常见于父母、房东、室友立规矩。

sourceContext.previousLine: Yes, yes, it is.
sourceContext.targetLine: No, but it's good. Emily thinks we should get all new stuff.
sourceContext.nextLine: Stuff that's just ours. Together, you know, brand new.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Emily 觉得我们应该把东西全换新的。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 离婚或分居后把 shared stuff 全换掉，是美剧里很典型的“重新开始”姿态。
zh-CN.usageNotes: stuff 是超高频口语集合名词，比 things 更松，比 belongings 更生活化。

sourceContext.previousLine: Aren't you supposed to be at an audition for another hour?
sourceContext.targetLine: Well, I'm sorry if I'm not a middle-aged black woman.
sourceContext.nextLine: And I'm also sorry if sometimes I go to the wrong audition.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 如果我不是个中年黑人大妈，那我也很抱歉啊。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Ross 模仿电视剧人物失败后自嘲，这句的笑点依赖美国电视刻板印象。认识这种身份标签组合，能更快听懂美式自嘲。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 这类身份词组本身不难，但一旦放进喜剧里，经常牵涉文化角色类型和模仿对象，不只是字面身份。

sourceContext.previousLine: And I'm also sorry if sometimes I go to the wrong audition.
sourceContext.targetLine: Okay, look, if I have to pretend I don't know about you two...
sourceContext.nextLine: ...then you two are gonna have to pretend there's nothing to know about.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你要是逼我假装不知道你们俩的事……
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Joey 被迫在知道秘密和装作不知情之间来回切换，这种 pretend I don't know 很适合描述社交装傻。
zh-CN.usageNotes: pretend 后面直接接句子在口语里很常见: pretend you didn't hear that / pretend I don't know。

source.episodeTitle: The One Where Ross Moves In

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One Where Ross Moves In

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E07 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: Larry?
sourceContext.targetLine: Yeah, I'd let him check out my kitchen floor.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 要是他肯，我都想好好看看他的厨房地板了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Monica 朋友在调情式夸 Ross 新邻居时，用 check out 暗示自己在“看货”。这里有明显的暧昧口气。
zh-CN.usageNotes: check out someone 常带“看上了、在打量”的意思；check out something 则更像“去看看”。

sourceContext.previousLine: I have one question.
sourceContext.targetLine: What is the deal with this?
sourceContext.nextLine: Bye-bye, little puppet Joey hand?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 这到底是怎么回事？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 看到 Ross 搬进来后的怪异状态，朋友第一反应就是追问“这什么情况”。这在吐槽型对话里特别实用。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 比 What happened? 更像带情绪的追问，常见于嫌弃、困惑、吐槽。

sourceContext.previousLine: Just go say hi.
sourceContext.targetLine: I have to go downstairs and back up, as if I'm coming home from the gala.
sourceContext.nextLine: So just go distract him. But don't be sexy.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我得先下楼，再倒回来，假装自己像是刚参加完晚会回家。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Ross 为了在新邻居面前自然出现，连走位都要重来一遍。back up 在生活场景里不只是倒车，也能说人往回退、重新走位。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 既能说车倒车，也能说人后退、撤一下。口语里还有“备份”义，但这里完全是动作义。

sourceContext.previousLine: - What? - He thought you said gonad.
sourceContext.targetLine: Wow, Monica. If every place were this clean, it'd be tough making a living.
sourceContext.nextLine: Ha, oh, Larry!

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Monica，要是每家都这么干净，我这行可就难混了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 清洁工拿 Monica 的洁癖开玩笑，making a living 点出的是“这就是我的饭碗”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 很常见的职场表达。比 earn money 更像“靠某种工作长期维生”。

sourceContext.previousLine: Wow, thirsty, huh?
sourceContext.targetLine: I'm having a party on Saturday. Sort of a housewarming thing.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Oh, fun. - Oh, great!

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我周六要办个派对，算是那种暖房聚会吧。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Ross 刚搬进新房，housewarming 是美国搬家后很典型的社交场景词。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 最标准说法是 housewarming party。thing 放进来会让语气更随意，像“算是那么个意思”。

sourceContext.previousLine: - Ha-ha-ha, it's okay to laugh? - I was just kidding.
sourceContext.targetLine: - I'll check the kitchen floor. - Knock yourself out.
sourceContext.nextLine: Yummy! Heh.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 去吧，随便看个够。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Monica 让人去看厨房地板时，表面客气，实际上带着一点无所谓的打趣。这个表达非常口语化。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 多用于“你想干就干吧”。语气从真心允许到轻微敷衍都可能，得听说话人的表情和语调。

sourceContext.previousLine: How's Ross doing since all the Emily stuff?
sourceContext.targetLine: He's not great. But, um, he's dealing with it.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Oh. - Wait, you're not...

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 他状态不算好，不过还在努力扛着。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Ross 搬进 Joey 和 Chandler 家后还在婚姻失败里缓慢恢复，这里的 dealing with it 带一种勉强运转的感觉。
zh-CN.usageNotes: deal with it 在第三人称里常表示“他还在处理这事”。不像 solve 那么彻底，更像持续应对。

source.episodeTitle: The One With The Thanksgiving Flashbacks

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One With The Thanksgiving Flashbacks

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E08 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: Am I more thankful for my divorce, or my eviction? Hmm.
sourceContext.targetLine: Wow, see, and I didn't think you'd be able to come up with anything.
sourceContext.nextLine: I'm sorry. It's just this is the worst Thanksgiving ever.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 哇，我还真没想到你居然能想出点什么。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 感恩节回忆局里，大家互相吐槽，come up with 在这里带一点“我本来不看好你”的损人味道。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 除了“想到主意”，也能表示临场编出借口、说辞。语气常跟夸奖或嘲讽一起出现。

sourceContext.previousLine: I put it on to scare Chandler.
sourceContext.targetLine: Oh, my God. Monica's going to totally freak out.
sourceContext.nextLine: Well, then help me get it off.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 天啊，Monica 肯定得炸了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Monica 对感恩节秩序感很强，所以别人一想到她会 freak out，观众立刻就能预判她的反应。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 说别人会 freak out，常带提前预警意味。比 get upset 更夸张、更有戏剧感。

sourceContext.previousLine: Um, and, you know, if I get really hot...
sourceContext.targetLine: ...um, I like to pick up this knife.
sourceContext.nextLine: And, um, I put the cool steel...

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我喜欢把这把刀拿起来……
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 回忆片段里，动作一开始就让观众知道危险要来了。pick up 在生活戏里非常基础，但一进厨房事故场景就瞬间关键。
zh-CN.usageNotes: pick up 是超高频动作短语。要注意它在不同语境里能表示拿起、接人、学会、恢复等很多义项。

sourceContext.previousLine: It wouldn't be Thanksgiving without Chandler bumming us out.
sourceContext.targetLine: It's a tradition, like the parade.
sourceContext.nextLine: If the parade decided it was gay, moved out and abandoned its entire family.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 这是传统啊，就跟游行一样。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 感恩节集里出现 tradition 很关键，因为美国人对节日仪式感非常强，这不是随口说说，而是在给行为找文化正当性。
zh-CN.usageNotes: It's a tradition 常用来为看似奇怪但“祖传”的做法辩护。后面常接 in our family / on Thanksgiving。

sourceContext.previousLine: Come on. I want to hear it.
sourceContext.targetLine: It wouldn't be Thanksgiving without Chandler bumming us out.
sourceContext.nextLine: It's a tradition, like the parade.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 要不是 Chandler 在，感恩节都不像感恩节了，他总得负责把我们气氛弄低。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这是老友之间对 Chandler“负责扫兴”的熟人吐槽。bum out 很口语，情绪比 depress 轻，但特别生活化。
zh-CN.usageNotes: something bums me out 指某事让我心情很 down。语气偏随意，不像 depress 那么重。

sourceContext.previousLine: ...and this lovely fall breeze came in out of nowhere...
sourceContext.targetLine: ...and blew this chick's skirt right up.
sourceContext.nextLine: Oh, which reminds me, I'm also thankful for thongs.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 那阵风一下就把那姑娘的裙子掀了起来。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这里的 right up 不是方向词，而是强化动作幅度: 裙子不是“有点起来”，而是直接被掀上去了。
zh-CN.usageNotes: right 在口语里常做强调副词，如 right here, right now, right up，表示“就、直接、完全”。

sourceContext.previousLine: Wow, see, and I didn't think you'd be able to come up with anything.
sourceContext.targetLine: I'm sorry. It's just this is the worst Thanksgiving ever.
sourceContext.nextLine: No, no, no. I am the king of bad Thanksgivings.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 对不起，我就是觉得这是有史以来最惨的一次感恩节。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Friends 很爱用 the worst ... ever 这种夸张句式来做喜剧升级，既口语又特别有表演感。
zh-CN.usageNotes: the best/worst thing ever 是美式口语高频夸张法。适合强烈吐槽，但正式场合不太用。

sourceContext.previousLine: Oh, my God. Monica's going to totally freak out.
sourceContext.targetLine: Well, then help me get it off.
sourceContext.nextLine: Plus, it smells really bad in here.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 那就来帮我把这玩意弄下来。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 火鸡卡头回忆里，get it off 就是最直接的求助动作词，听懂了就能立刻跟上画面。
zh-CN.usageNotes: get something off 语义很广，常见于脱衣服、把东西摘下来、清掉污渍等动作场景。

source.episodeTitle: The One With Ross's Sandwich

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One With Ross's Sandwich

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E09 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: Are you just repeating me?
sourceContext.targetLine: All right. Let's move on.
sourceContext.nextLine: Okay, then.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 好了，我们继续往下说。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这里不是感情翻篇，而是会议/讨论里主持节奏的 move on。中级学习者很容易只记 breakup 义，听力里反而漏掉这种高频功能义。
zh-CN.usageNotes: move on to the next topic / let's move on 都很常见。它经常出现在工作会议、课堂和朋友讨论里。

sourceContext.previousLine: Get ready to come out of the non-gay closet.
sourceContext.targetLine: I promise to come up with something. Just give us time.
sourceContext.nextLine: All right.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我保证会想出办法的，再给我们点时间。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Monica 和 Chandler 需要赶紧编出解释，come up with 在这里是危机处理里的“快想个说法”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 除了 idea / plan，也常接 excuse, explanation, cover story 这类应急说辞。

sourceContext.previousLine: ...my sandwich!
sourceContext.targetLine: Now calm down.
sourceContext.nextLine: Look in my office. Some may be in the trash.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 先冷静点。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Ross 的三明治被吃后情绪瞬间失控，这句 calm down 是别人试图拉住他别继续升级。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 这种短句常见但不总是有效。对方正在暴怒时，说 calm down 容易火上浇油。

sourceContext.previousLine: - Hey. - Hey.
sourceContext.targetLine: Since that video camera thing didn't work out...
sourceContext.nextLine: ...I thought that I would give you just a little preview.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 既然录像机那招没成……
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这里说的是某个计划没有成功，不是感情线。work out 是典型多义词，这种“方案没奏效”的义项特别值得中级学习者补齐。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 谈方案、安排、尝试时，didn't work out = 没成、没奏效。不要只记“锻炼”那个义项。

sourceContext.previousLine: Uh, Monica?
sourceContext.targetLine: I guess I set up the video camera to try and entice Joey.
sourceContext.nextLine: But, sadly, I could not be enticed.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我想我是把录像机架好，想把 Joey 引过去。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: set up 在这里是实际把设备摆好、开好。Friends 里这类家居动作词特别多，听懂它们很能提升看剧顺畅度。
zh-CN.usageNotes: set up 既能说搭设备，也能说安排约会、设局、成立组织。要靠后面的宾语判断。

sourceContext.previousLine: What's wrong, buddy?
sourceContext.targetLine: Someone at work ate my sandwich.
sourceContext.nextLine: What did the police say?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 公司里居然有人把我的三明治吃了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句之所以好学，不是因为 sandwich 难，而是它直接触发 Ross 的职场崩溃。美剧里“动我吃的”经常是严重冒犯。
zh-CN.usageNotes: eat my lunch / eat my sandwich 这类结构也可引申成“抢我机会、占我便宜”，具体要看语境。

sourceContext.previousLine: We'll be more careful.
sourceContext.targetLine: We don't want them to know, because it's going really well.
sourceContext.nextLine: And maybe it's going well because it's a secret.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我们不想让他们知道，因为现在进展真的很顺。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Monica 和 Chandler 秘密恋爱正处在蜜月推进期，go well 是口语里评价一段关系或计划状态最自然的说法之一。
zh-CN.usageNotes: How's it going? It's going well. 这套说法在关系、项目、面试、康复场景里都高频。

sourceContext.previousLine: Thank you, Joey, so much.
sourceContext.targetLine: You're not welcome, okay?
sourceContext.nextLine: Look, I hate this. You guys keep embarrassing me.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你一点也不值得谢，好吗？这里不欢迎你。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Ross 对偷吃三明治的人彻底翻脸，not welcome 在这里就是明确赶人、断绝好脸色。
zh-CN.usageNotes: You're not welcome here 很强硬，比 leave 更像“你以后也别指望被好好对待”。

source.episodeTitle: The One With The Inappropriate Sister

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One With The Inappropriate Sister

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E10 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: I'll help you.
sourceContext.targetLine: Yeah. I'll make up a schedule and make sure you stick to it.
sourceContext.nextLine: Plus it'll give me something to do.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 行，我给你排个计划表，确保你照着执行。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Monica 一进入“管理模式”，就不是随口提建议，而是直接 make up a schedule。很符合她控制型人格。
zh-CN.usageNotes: make up 在这里不是“和好”，而是“编出来/拟出来”。和 schedule, story, excuse 搭配都很常见。

sourceContext.previousLine: We'll start off slow. The only thing you have to do tonight...
sourceContext.targetLine: ...is come up with the name of your main character.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Done. - And it can't be Joey.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 先想出你主角的名字。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Joey 教别人写剧本时，先从 come up with a name 这种最基础的创作动作讲起。
zh-CN.usageNotes: come up with 后面接具体产物时，常暗示“从零想出来”，比如名字、点子、借口、方案。

sourceContext.previousLine: What?
sourceContext.targetLine: - Uh, Ginger's gonna take over this corner. - That chick can't handle my corner.
sourceContext.nextLine: Look, either you leave or we remove you.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Ginger 要接管这个街角了。那妞可守不住我的地盘。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 街头卖艺/拉客的地盘意识很强，所以 take over the corner 听起来像在抢场子。
zh-CN.usageNotes: take over 常见于工作交接、公司收购、轮班顶上，也能像这里一样说“把某块地盘接过去”。

sourceContext.previousLine: I don't know what you two are talking about, but I'd just like to say thanks to both of you.
sourceContext.targetLine: You. You wouldn't let me give up on myself.
sourceContext.nextLine: And you. Well, you co-created Fireball.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 是你啊，你没有让我对自己放弃。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句不是普通的 give up，而是带 on myself 的情感重量: 别人没放弃我，所以我也没彻底垮掉。
zh-CN.usageNotes: give up on someone/something 是非常值得学的结构，表示“不再相信还有希望”。

sourceContext.previousLine: I am not unemployed. I'm on sabbatical.
sourceContext.targetLine: Hey, don't get religious on me, okay?
sourceContext.nextLine: A guy in your position needs to be a little better at relaxing, you know.

definitions: 别突然跟我讲宗教大道理
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 嘿，你可别突然跟我来这套宗教味儿，好吗？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Joey 不想听别人上价值，所以用 get religious on me 把“突然开始讲神神叨叨的道理”说得很口语。
zh-CN.usageNotes: get ... on me 是很美式的抱怨结构，表示“你别突然对我来这一套”。

sourceContext.previousLine: Uh, okay. Is there anything else?
sourceContext.targetLine: Well, you're just gonna say no again, but...
sourceContext.nextLine: ...gay porn?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你肯定还是会再拒绝我，不过……
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 约人被拒过的人再开口时，很容易用 say no again 这种预设失败的说法先自我保护。
zh-CN.usageNotes: say yes / say no 是非常口语的“答应/拒绝”。again 放进去后，会有明显的情绪包袱。

sourceContext.previousLine: They gave me a great spot right by Macy's.
sourceContext.targetLine: They never give such a good corner to a rookie...
sourceContext.nextLine: ...but I'm the only one who can say "Merry Christmas" in 25 languages.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 他们从来不会把这么好的街角给一个新人……
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 职业等级感在这句里非常明显。rookie 不是单纯“新”，而是“资历浅、还没坐稳位置”的人。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 体育、职场、警局等层级明确的环境里特别常见。比 newcomer 更带行业内部视角。

source.episodeTitle: The One With All The Resolutions

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One With All The Resolutions

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E11 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: What? I don't gossip.
sourceContext.targetLine: Maybe sometimes I find out things.
sourceContext.nextLine: Or I hear something and I pass that information on.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 有时候我就是会打听到一些事。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 为自己爱传消息辩护时，把 gossip 先包装成“我只是会知道事情”。这正是英语里很自然的自我美化说法。
zh-CN.usageNotes: find out 既能说自己主动查到，也能说后来得知。比 know 更强调信息是后来获得的。

sourceContext.previousLine: And you're not supposed to be gossiping.
sourceContext.targetLine: No, I know, I can't keep this in. So I pick up the phone...
sourceContext.nextLine: I'm not listening to you.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我知道，我憋不住，所以我就拿起电话……
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 忍不住八卦时，pick up the phone 就是“冲动行动”的起点。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 现在手机时代依然高频。它不只是物理拿起，更常指“主动打电话”。

sourceContext.previousLine: Aw.
sourceContext.targetLine: Ross is wearing leather pants.
sourceContext.nextLine: Does nobody else see that Ross is wearing leather pants?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Ross 穿着皮裤呢。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 本集最经典的社死道具之一。这个词组本身不难，但它在剧情里承载的是“自我形象翻车”的笑点。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 看剧时要把道具词和剧情记忆绑在一起。leather pants 常自带“想很酷，结果很惨”的喜剧色彩。

sourceContext.previousLine: Maybe sometimes I find out things.
sourceContext.targetLine: Or I hear something and I pass that information on.
sourceContext.nextLine: You know, kind of like a public service.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 或者我听到什么，就把那消息再转给别人。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 用 pass that information on 给“传八卦”换了个体面说法，特别像成年人给自己找借口。
zh-CN.usageNotes: pass something on 很高频，既能传消息，也能传东西、传建议。比 spread gossip 中性得多。

sourceContext.previousLine: Listen, who are you kissing at midnight? Rachel or Phoebe?
sourceContext.targetLine: - What? - Well, you gotta kiss someone.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Can't kiss your sister. - Who's gonna kiss my sister?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 那你总得亲个人吧。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 新年决议游戏里，gotta 带的是轻松施压: 规则摆在那儿，你别想躲。
zh-CN.usageNotes: gotta = have got to，极口语。很适合表达“总得、必须得”，比 must 轻松很多。

sourceContext.previousLine: 1 Is there any entertainment there?
sourceContext.targetLine: All right. Here we go. 1999, the year of Joey.
sourceContext.nextLine: We're very happy for you.

definitions: Joey 的年份；Joey 要开挂的一年
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 好了，开始吧。1999 年，Joey 的一年到了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Joey 给自己立人设时，用 the year of Joey 这种很美式、很自我打气的说法给新年定调。
zh-CN.usageNotes: the year of me / the summer of George 这类表达常用来半开玩笑地宣布“接下来是我的主场”。

sourceContext.previousLine: Hey, Rach, maybe your resolution should be to, um, gossip less.
sourceContext.targetLine: What? I don't gossip.
sourceContext.nextLine: Maybe sometimes I find out things.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 什么？我才不八卦呢。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 明明就在传播信息，却急着撇清 gossip 这个标签。这个词带明显社交判断，不只是“聊天”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: gossip 既可作名词也可作动词。说 I don't gossip 往往说明说话人其实很在意自己显得不厚道。

source.episodeTitle: The One With Chandler's Work Laugh

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One With Chandler's Work Laugh

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E12 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: Did they teach you that in anger management?
sourceContext.targetLine: You know what might help deal with it? Think of it this way.
sourceContext.nextLine: You and Emily are in the past. You can't be mad about the past.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你知道什么也许能帮你熬过去吗？换个角度想。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Chandler 不想参加工作饭局，Monica 先用 deal with it 定调: 这事逃不了，只能想办法消化。
zh-CN.usageNotes: deal with it 不一定是粗暴怼人，也可以像这里一样是“帮你想个能熬过去的方法”。

sourceContext.previousLine: - Oh, that was my work laugh. - Your work laugh?
sourceContext.targetLine: If you're going to survive this party, you have to come up with one too.
sourceContext.nextLine: - All right. Check me out. - Okay.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你要是想熬过这场派对，也得给自己编一个笑出来。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Monica 在教 Chandler 如何应对办公室社交假笑，本质上是在教他现场“造一个”出来。
zh-CN.usageNotes: come up with one 这种 one 指代前文内容的说法很常见，听力里要能快速接住省略。

sourceContext.previousLine: So I laugh at my boss's jokes. What's the big deal?
sourceContext.targetLine: I'd rather not hang out with this work-weasel guy...
sourceContext.nextLine: ...when I could be with my boyfriend, who I actually respect.

definitions: 职场黄鼠狼；办公室小人
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我宁可别跟那个办公室黄鼠狼一起混。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Monica 一开口就把 Chandler 的同事打成 work-weasel，说明她对这种油滑型职场人很反感。
zh-CN.usageNotes: weasel 本身就常用来骂人狡猾、滑头。前面加 work-，立刻变成职场语境里的损人标签。

sourceContext.previousLine: - Oh! I know one thing. - What?
sourceContext.targetLine: They did it right there on the couch.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Hey, Ross. - Yeah.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 他们就在那张沙发上做的。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: right there 在这里不是简单地点词，而是在强调“就在眼前、就在那个地方”，所以冲击感更强。
zh-CN.usageNotes: right there 很高频，可表示“就在那儿”“当场”“明摆着”。语气靠重音决定。

sourceContext.previousLine: What are you doing here? I thought you had inventory.
sourceContext.targetLine: Well I do, but I decided I'd take a long lunch...
sourceContext.nextLine: ...and spend some time with my friend, Monica.

definitions: 午休拖得很长；摸鱼加长午休
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我本来也知道，但我决定给自己来个超长午休。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 职场人在烦躁或想逃避时说 take a long lunch，很有美式上班族的那种被动反抗味道。
zh-CN.usageNotes: take lunch / take a long lunch 常用于办公室场景，意思不是认真吃饭，而是把中午那段时间拉长。

sourceContext.previousLine: You know what might help deal with it? Think of it this way.
sourceContext.targetLine: You and Emily are in the past. You can't be mad about the past.
sourceContext.nextLine: Are you still mad about, you know, the Louisiana Purchase?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你和 Emily 都已经过去了，你没法一直为过去生气。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Monica 用 in the past 劝 Ross 别再纠缠旧账，这比 just forget it 更像“把那段关系归档”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: leave it in the past / that's in the past 都很常见。适合谈旧关系、旧冲突、旧错误。

source.episodeTitle: The One With Joey's Bag

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One With Joey's Bag

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E13 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: We were in the market...
sourceContext.targetLine: ...and she bent down to get some yogurt and never came back up again.
sourceContext.nextLine: I'm so sorry.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 她弯腰去拿酸奶，然后就再也没起来过。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Joey 讲笑话时用 came back up 做夸张收尾，画面感很强，也带点老派段子节奏。
zh-CN.usageNotes: come back up 本义是“回到上面来”，放进笑话里常被用来制造夸张反差。

sourceContext.previousLine: I made up a song to sing to you, but that made you cry even more.
sourceContext.targetLine: You make up songs?
sourceContext.nextLine: Well, no, just that one. But it was stupid.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你还会现场编歌啊？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 的创作方式就是典型的 make up songs: 不是正式创作，而是想到哪唱到哪。
zh-CN.usageNotes: make up 在这里是“临时编”。跟 songs, stories, excuses 搭配都很常见。

sourceContext.previousLine: You know what? It was a stupid play anyway.
sourceContext.targetLine: It's time to give up the bag.
sourceContext.nextLine: I don't want to give up the bag.

definitions: 把包放弃掉；别再背这个包了
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 是时候跟这个包说再见了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Joey 对男包上头后，朋友们终于开始劝退。give up the bag 的重点是“放弃这个自我感觉良好的错误选择”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: give up 后面直接接具体物品时，往往带有“别再执着这个东西了”的语气。

sourceContext.previousLine: - Okay. - All right.
sourceContext.targetLine: I don't like to brag about it, but I give the best massages.
sourceContext.nextLine: All right, then massage me up right nice!

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我平时不爱炫耀，但我按摩是真的最好。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 典型的“嘴上说不炫，身体很诚实”。brag about it 正是这种先装谦虚再自夸的口语套路。
zh-CN.usageNotes: brag about 后面常接 money, connections, skills。自带一点让人翻白眼的意味。

sourceContext.previousLine: - Okay. - All right.
sourceContext.targetLine: I don't like to brag about it, but I give the best massages.
sourceContext.nextLine: All right, then massage me up right nice!

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我不爱炫耀，但我做按摩真的是一绝。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 在职业技能上非常自信，所以会直接说自己 gives the best massages。这种说法很适合学“自夸技能”时的英语表达。
zh-CN.usageNotes: give a massage / give the best massages 里用 give，不用 do。很多学习者容易被中文直译带跑。

sourceContext.targetLine: 1 Ah, I can't believe we've never done this. It's so good!
sourceContext.nextLine: It's so good for Monica.

definitions: 我们居然以前都没这样过
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 天啊，我真不敢信我们以前从没这样过，太舒服了！
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Monica 和 Chandler 发现一起做某件小事也能很亲密，这种 we've never done this 带有新鲜感和情侣亲密升级。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 很适合描述第一次一起尝试的事。后面常接 before，或直接省掉靠语境补全。

source.episodeTitle: The One Where Everybody Finds Out

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One Where Everybody Finds Out

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E14 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: You know what you should do?
sourceContext.targetLine: You should find out what his hobbies are and then use that to bond with him.
sourceContext.nextLine: Yeah. Like if I wanted something from Joey...

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你该先搞清楚他平时都有什么爱好，再拿这个跟他套近乎。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 在教 Rachel 怎么和 Ugly Naked Guy 套近乎。find out 在这里就是“先摸底，再社交出击”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: find out what his hobbies are 这种 wh- 从句结构特别常见，听熟了能明显提升对话理解。

sourceContext.previousLine: So he likes to break stuff.
sourceContext.targetLine: Okay, I've got to go pick up Ben, but I will figure something out.
sourceContext.nextLine: Hey, didn't he used to have a cat?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我得先去接 Ben，不过我会想出办法的。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 美国亲子场景里，pick up kids 是极高频表达，不只是“拿起来”，而是固定的接送语义。
zh-CN.usageNotes: pick up the kids / pick Ben up 在家长、伴侣、朋友代接场景里都很常见。

sourceContext.previousLine: Oh, heh.
sourceContext.targetLine: Well, I never thought I'd say this, but I'm gonna go use Ugly Naked Guy's bathroom.
sourceContext.nextLine: Oh, look. There's Monica and Chandler. Hey!

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我真没想到我会说这话，但我要去借用一下 Ugly Naked Guy 的厕所。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句好笑的点在于: 为了窥探/接近邻居，Rachel 连这种台阶都想出来了。use someone's bathroom 是很实用的生活表达。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 比 go to the bathroom 更具体，因为它强调“借用某人的洗手间”。社交里很常用。

sourceContext.previousLine: Actually, we saw them doing it up against the window.
sourceContext.targetLine: Okay, so now they know you know, and they don't know Rachel knows?
sourceContext.nextLine: Yes, but you know what? It doesn't matter who knows what.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 所以现在他们知道你已经知道了，可他们还不知道 Rachel 也知道？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 本集秘密链条层层套娃，know you know 这种嵌套式表达是剧情笑点核心。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 英语里这种重复 know 的结构很常见，听的时候不要被绕晕，本质上是在追踪信息层级。

sourceContext.previousLine: Ironically, most of the boxes seem to be labeled "Clothes."
sourceContext.targetLine: Aw, I'm going to miss that big old squishy butt.
sourceContext.nextLine: And we're done with the chicken fried rice.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 啊，我会想念那一大坨软乎乎的屁股的。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Friends 很爱用 big old + 名词 这种夸张又有点嫌弃的口语结构，既形象又带喜剧感。
zh-CN.usageNotes: big old car / big old house / big old squishy butt 都是同一套路，old 在这里不是年龄，而是加强语气。

sourceContext.previousLine: Isn't it perfect?
sourceContext.targetLine: I can't believe I never realized how great it is.
sourceContext.nextLine: That is because your eye immediately goes to the big naked man.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我真不敢信我以前从没意识到这样有多爽。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Rachel 借机会在暧昧计划里越玩越上头，never realized 把“之前一直没开窍”说得很自然。
zh-CN.usageNotes: I never realized how + 形容词/句子 是非常实用的感叹结构，表示后来才突然发现。

term.text: right there in front of you

sourceContext.previousLine: ...and you know how sometimes you're looking for something...
sourceContext.targetLine: ...and you don't see that it's right there in front of you, sipping coffee...
sourceContext.nextLine: Oh, no. Have I said too much?

zh-CN.corePhrase: right there in front of you
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: ……可你就是看不出来，它明明就在你眼前，还在那儿喝咖啡呢。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 疯狂暗示 Ross“真爱就在眼前”，right there in front of you 把“你怎么还看不见”这层急切推满了。
zh-CN.usageNotes: right there 本身就能强调“明摆着”。加 in front of you 后，指责感和戏剧感都会更强。

source.episodeTitle: The One With The Girl Who Hits Joey

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One With The Girl Who Hits Joey

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E15 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: Okay? I'm in love with her.
sourceContext.targetLine: I'm so sorry that you had to find out this way.
sourceContext.nextLine: I'm sorry, but it's true. I love him too.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 真对不起，让你以这种方式知道这件事。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句是英语里非常常见的“抱歉你是这样被通知的”模板，适合秘密曝光、坏消息、尴尬真相被撞破。
zh-CN.usageNotes: I'm sorry you had to find out this way 几乎是固定句型，记整块最省事。

sourceContext.previousLine: Yeah, so? What's that supposed to mean?
sourceContext.targetLine: Hey, Chandler, don't freak out. I'm telling you something you already know.
sourceContext.nextLine: Come on. She broke up with Richard because he didn't want to have babies.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Chandler，别炸毛，我告诉你的其实是你早就知道的事。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 朋友在说大消息前先压一下对方情绪，freak out 在这里带着半开玩笑、半认真预防失控的意味。
zh-CN.usageNotes: Don't freak out, but... 是高频开头，基本等于“我接下来要说的可能会吓你一跳”。

sourceContext.previousLine: - What? - That's what we do.
sourceContext.targetLine: I mess up, you tell me how to fix it and I do.
sourceContext.nextLine: And then, you know, you think I'm all cute again.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 要是我搞砸了，你告诉我怎么补救，我就照做。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Joey 的感情求助很实在: 我搞砸，你给我修复方案。这正是 mess up 在亲密关系里常见的用法。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 比 make a mistake 更口语，也更带后果感。mess up a relationship / an audition / a chance 都常见。

sourceContext.previousLine: Yeah, go back to 3-B, 3-B.
sourceContext.targetLine: All right, everyone, calm down. Everyone, calm down.
sourceContext.nextLine: I have something that I would like to say.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 好了，大家冷静，大家都冷静。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 群体场面失控时，重复 calm down 是很真实的控场方式。重复本身就说明场面已经不好收了。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 对一群人讲话时常直接用 everyone, calm down。越重复通常说明越没控住。

sourceContext.previousLine: You know when I said that I want you...
sourceContext.targetLine: ...to deal with relationship stuff all on your own?
sourceContext.nextLine: - Well, you're not ready for that. - I didn't think I was.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: ……独自去处理那些恋爱关系里的事？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Joey 把复杂的情侣问题一概打包成 relationship stuff，很符合男性朋友间那种不细讲、但都懂的口语压缩法。
zh-CN.usageNotes: stuff 在这里特别好用，能把一堆不想展开的细节统称掉。口语里很自然。

sourceContext.previousLine: I saw what you were doing to my sister, now, get out here!
sourceContext.targetLine: Well, listen, we had a good run.
sourceContext.nextLine: You know? What was it? Four, five months?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 听着，我们这一段其实也算过得挺好了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 说关系结束时用 a good run，很美式，也很会给这段经历留体面，不会说得太惨。
zh-CN.usageNotes: have a good run 可用于感情、工作、节目、球队。意思是“有过一段不错的时期”。

sourceContext.previousLine: Sure. If you cared enough to call it first.
sourceContext.targetLine: - Guys, you're a few steps ahead of us. - Yeah, big zero-gravity moon steps.
sourceContext.nextLine: Oh, oh, I just thought of the greatest wedding gift to get you.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你们几个已经领先我们好几步了。对，还是那种零重力登月步。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这句把“进度差”说得既直观又好笑，尤其适合感情、计划、理解程度被别人甩开的语境。
zh-CN.usageNotes: be one step ahead / be a few steps ahead 都很常见，既能说真领先，也能说心智或信息差。

source.episodeTitle: The One With The Cop

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One With The Cop

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E16 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: Can you discover the badge again?
sourceContext.targetLine: I think I can come up with something better than that.
sourceContext.nextLine: I bet somebody's missing that badge.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我觉得我能想出个比那更好的办法。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Ross 又开始动脑解决尴尬局面时，这句就是典型的“先否掉旧方案，再立刻自信接管”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: something better 是很顺手的补语。工作、约会、借口、礼物都能这么说。

sourceContext.previousLine: Pivot!
sourceContext.targetLine: Shut up! Shut up! Shut up!
sourceContext.nextLine: I don't think it's gonna pivot anymore.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 闭嘴！闭嘴！闭嘴！
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 情绪一上来，直接连发 shut up。Friends 里它常是熟人间爆笑式制止，不一定真有恶意。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 语气跨度很大。对朋友能是夸张反应，对陌生人则相当不礼貌，要注意场合。

sourceContext.previousLine: No, no. No.
sourceContext.targetLine: It's going to be all smelling like Monica.
sourceContext.nextLine: Are you saying I smell bad?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 这沙发以后闻起来全都会是 Monica 的味道。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Gary 在认真想象共同生活的细节时，连 couch 会 smell like Monica 都觉得甜。
zh-CN.usageNotes: smell like 后面既能接食物、地方，也能接人。用在人身上时常带亲密感或嫌弃感。

sourceContext.previousLine: I still don't know.
sourceContext.targetLine: I'm sorry, I just want to make sure I bought the right couch, one that says:
sourceContext.nextLine: "Kids welcome here," but that also says

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我就是想确认我买对了那张沙发，那种一看就会说……
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Gary 把一张 couch 当成关系信号来挑，right 在这里是“适合两人生活”的对味，不只是“正确”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: the right + 名词 很高频，表示“最合适的那个”，不等于唯一正确答案。

sourceContext.previousLine: But are we ever gonna have the closeness like you guys have?
sourceContext.targetLine: Monica and I were friends before we started dating. Maybe that's it.
sourceContext.nextLine: Friends first?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Monica 和我是在正式开始交往之前就已经是朋友了，也许关键就在这儿。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Ross 在分析情侣为什么能合得来时，start dating 和 before that 的时间线意识很重要。
zh-CN.usageNotes: start dating 表示开始正式以恋爱方式见面。比 go out with 稍微明确一点。

sourceContext.previousLine: - Oh, I can do it. - Yeah.
sourceContext.targetLine: You two will really enjoy that couch.
sourceContext.nextLine: We're, uh, not together.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你们俩一定会很享受那张沙发的。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 旁人用 really enjoy that couch 疯狂暗示“情侣用途”，说得一本正经反而更好笑。
zh-CN.usageNotes: really enjoy 比 like 更饱满。谈物件时常带“这东西会成为你们生活的一部分”的意思。

source.episodeTitle: The One With Rachel's Inadvertent Kiss

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One With Rachel's Inadvertent Kiss

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E17 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: Okay, you know where you are better than I do. I was just curious.
sourceContext.targetLine: Why don't you just calm down, Phoebe, all right?
sourceContext.nextLine: Get all your facts before you tell everybody you're the only hot couple.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你先冷静点，Phoebe，行吗？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 一激动就往最坏处想，别人只能先按情绪，再谈事实。calm down 在熟人关系里就是最直接的情绪刹车。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 句尾加 all right? 会让语气更像安抚，也可能更像不耐烦，得看口气。

sourceContext.previousLine: One, two, three. Got it!
sourceContext.targetLine: I gotta check out this hot girl.
sourceContext.nextLine: There she is!

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我得去看看那个辣妹。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Joey 一听说楼里有美女，立刻说要去 check out。这里就是很典型的 Joey 式猎艳雷达。
zh-CN.usageNotes: check out someone 常带“带着兴趣去看”的语气，谈吸引对象时尤其明显。

sourceContext.previousLine: What are you doing?
sourceContext.targetLine: Um, well, same thing we did all day. Just hang out at Gary's apartment.
sourceContext.nextLine: He is so amazing. We never left the bedroom.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 嗯，还是跟白天一样，就在 Gary 家里待着。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Rachel 口中的 hang out 带出情侣初期那种“没安排也愿意一起耗着”的亲密感。
zh-CN.usageNotes: hang out with someone 往往比 go on a date 更轻、更自然，但恋爱里也常是升温信号。

sourceContext.previousLine: Monica, this hot girl in Ross' building is flirting with me.
sourceContext.targetLine: Get in there, man. Flirt back. Mix it up!
sourceContext.nextLine: Yeah, I'm down with that.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 进去啊，兄弟。回撩她，别只会傻站着，灵活点！
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Joey 教 Ross 把对方递过来的暧昧球接回去。flirt back 很适合约会和社交判断场景。
zh-CN.usageNotes: flirt 本身可及物也可不及物。加 back 后强调“对方先来，我再回”。

sourceContext.previousLine: Let's just say I'm glad I'm not Chandler.
sourceContext.targetLine: That's right, Ross. I can see you in your new apartment. You see me.
sourceContext.nextLine: Same as yesterday, same as the day before.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我明天要去 Ralph Lauren 面试。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Rachel 终于摸到时尚行业门口，job interview 在这里不仅是事件词，也是她职业转向的重要剧情锚点。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 最自然的搭配是 have a job interview / go on an interview。不要只会说 do an interview。

sourceContext.previousLine: Anyway, I'm gonna be the coordinator of the women's collection.
sourceContext.targetLine: I'll work right under the director. It's the perfect job for me.
sourceContext.nextLine: Wow. Well, if you nail the interview, you'll get it.

definitions: 直接归导演管；就在导演手下
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我会直接在总监下面工作，这工作简直太适合我了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Rachel 说 right under the director 时，重点是离核心决策层很近，职业含金量一下就出来了。
zh-CN.usageNotes: work under someone 表示在某人手下做事。加 right 会强化“直接归这个人管”。

source.episodeTitle: The One Where Rachel Smokes

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One Where Rachel Smokes

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E18 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: You know, I rued the day once. Didn't get a whole lot else done.
sourceContext.targetLine: Okay, time to bring up the rest of the cups.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Hey, Joey. - Hey, Pheebs.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 好了，该把剩下那些杯子也搬上来了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Rachel 和 Monica 准备宴客时，bring up 是最生活化的搬运动作词。听懂这种短语，居家场景就会顺很多。
zh-CN.usageNotes: bring something up 既能字面“拿上来”，也能抽象“提起话题”。两个义项都超高频。

sourceContext.previousLine: Come on!
sourceContext.targetLine: You know what? We need to move on.
sourceContext.nextLine: Oh, no, I could do it one more time.

definitions: 继续别的了；别纠结这事
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你知道吗？我们得往前看了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 当一个办法走不通，Monica 会迅速切换方案。move on 在这里体现的是她很强的推进感。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 面对计划受阻、聊天卡壳、感情翻篇时都能说 move on，语域很广。

sourceContext.previousLine: I thought we were the Patch Sisters.
sourceContext.targetLine: Yeah, that didn't work out.
sourceContext.nextLine: Rachel, we tried to quit. But it was too hard.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 对啊，那招没成。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 这里是方案失败义，不是关系义。work out 的多义性在 Friends 这种快节奏对白里很容易误判。
zh-CN.usageNotes: didn't work out 可说关系没成、计划失败、时间不合适，得靠上下文判断。

sourceContext.previousLine: Come on, give me one of those. What are we talking about?
sourceContext.targetLine: No, you're doing great. Don't give up.
sourceContext.nextLine: That's why we didn't tell you, and we are not gonna drag you down.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 没有，你表现得很好，别放弃。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 安慰朋友试镜失败时，give up 是最直给的打气词，不绕弯，但很有效。
zh-CN.usageNotes: Don't give up 是鼓励型口语核心表达。正式场合也能用，不会显得太随便。

sourceContext.previousLine: What kind of an actor can't even say, "Mm, noodle soup"?
sourceContext.targetLine: Hey, you know what? Maybe you didn't mess up your audition because you suck.
sourceContext.nextLine: Maybe you messed up because you care more about your godson.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 也许你试镜搞砸，不是因为你真的不行。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Joey 需要被安慰时，朋友先把“你是不是烂”换成“是不是那次 audition 出了问题”，更容易被接受。
zh-CN.usageNotes: mess up 很适合搭 audition, interview, date, chance 这类“一次性关键机会”。

sourceContext.previousLine: Whoa.
sourceContext.targetLine: The guy's like the biggest commercial casting director in town.
sourceContext.nextLine: Ben takes one lousy walk in the park and gets an audition?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 那家伙可是城里最顶的广告选角导演之一。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 娱乐行业里，casting director 直接决定你有没有被看见的机会，所以 Joey 才会这么看重。
zh-CN.usageNotes: casting director 是影视行业核心岗位词。加 commercial 后，说明是广告不是电影或戏剧领域。

sourceContext.previousLine: The guy's like the biggest commercial casting director in town.
sourceContext.targetLine: Ben takes one lousy walk in the park and gets an audition?
sourceContext.nextLine: I mean, way to go, Ben!

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Ben 不就随便在公园走了一圈，居然就拿到一个试镜？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Joey 把机会稀缺感说得很酸，audition 在演员职业线里就是最核心的门槛词。
zh-CN.usageNotes: have an audition / get an audition / go to an audition 都很常见。别和 interview 混用。

source.episodeTitle: The One Where Ross Can't Flirt

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One Where Ross Can't Flirt

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E19 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: Rachel Karen Green, where's the other earring?
sourceContext.targetLine: Okay, okay. Look, just don't freak out, but I kind of lost it.
sourceContext.nextLine: Oh, I know it's in the apartment, but I definitely lost it.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 好好好，你先别慌，但我好像有点搞丢了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 说坏消息前先来一句 don't freak out，是很美式、也很危险的铺垫，因为通常只会让对方更紧张。
zh-CN.usageNotes: Don't freak out, but... 基本等于“接下来有雷”。记整块很划算。

sourceContext.previousLine: Those are my sunglasses. You borrowed them from me.
sourceContext.targetLine: Okay. Calm down. Here they are.
sourceContext.nextLine: Phoebe.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 好了，冷静，找到了，在这儿呢。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 东西一度找不到时，先说 calm down 再把实物递出来，是很真实的生活口语节奏。
zh-CN.usageNotes: calm down 后面常直接跟解决方案或新信息，像是在说“先别炸，问题已经解决了”。

sourceContext.previousLine: Made a few calls, pulled some strings...
sourceContext.targetLine: ...and they'll seat us if we both have chicken and don't get dessert.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Hi, Chandler. - Hey, Caitlin.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 只要我们俩都点鸡肉、不点甜点，他们就会给我们安排位子。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 纽约餐厅里排队等位是常态，seat us 直接是“餐厅愿不愿意给你桌”的关键动作。
zh-CN.usageNotes: restaurant staff seats you。这里的 seat 不是名词，而是“给人安排座位”的动词。

sourceContext.previousLine: Somebody got a haircut.
sourceContext.targetLine: Ugh. I hate it. I look like an 8-year-old boy.
sourceContext.nextLine: If that was true, gym class would've been more interesting.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: ……只要我们都点鸡肉，而且别再点甜点。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 为了省钱或凑最低消费，连 get dessert 都成了策略的一部分，很符合年轻人约会时的盘算感。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 在餐厅里 get 常常直接表示“点/要一份”。比 order 更口语、更轻。

sourceContext.previousLine: What the hell was that?
sourceContext.targetLine: - What? - The flirting.
sourceContext.nextLine: Aren't you supposed to be going out with, hmm, let's see, my sister?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 什么？就你那套撩人的表现啊。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Caitlin 一眼看穿 Ross 的问题不在话题，而在 flirting 本身太僵。这种把动词 nominalize 成 the flirting 的说法很口语。
zh-CN.usageNotes: flirting 可当名词，指整体的调情状态或操作，不只是单次 flirt。

sourceContext.previousLine: That was some hot love you gave me. Ha-ha-ha.
sourceContext.targetLine: - I'll get ready. - Wear those earrings I got you.
sourceContext.nextLine: Oh, that's a great idea. I was saving them for something special.

definitions: 戴上我送你的那副耳环
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我去准备。把我送你的那副耳环戴上。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Chandler 对 Monica 下达细节指令时，那种情侣间熟到能直接安排造型的感觉很自然。
zh-CN.usageNotes: wear 在口语里直接说穿戴状态，不需要额外补 on。those earrings 强调双方都知道是哪一副。

sourceContext.previousLine: ...the guy who can do something about it.
sourceContext.targetLine: Sounds like someone wants to be Mr. Pizza Delivery Girl.
sourceContext.nextLine: Well...

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 听起来某人是想当披萨外送妹的 Mr. Right 啊。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Joey 用 Pizza Delivery Girl 打趣 Ross 对女送餐员一见钟情。日常职业标签在暧昧玩笑里特别常见。
zh-CN.usageNotes: delivery girl/guy 这种说法很口语，但也稍微过时。真实场合更中性的说法是 delivery person。

source.episodeTitle: The One With The Ride Along

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One With The Ride Along

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E20 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: Who would buy this?
sourceContext.targetLine: Rach, it's me. Pick up.
sourceContext.nextLine: What's up?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: Rach，是我，快接电话。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 留言机时代，pick up 直接决定你是继续对机器说还是能跟真人说上话。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 电话语境里的 pick up 非常高频。现在依然常说 pick up the phone。

sourceContext.previousLine: And what's he gonna do?
sourceContext.targetLine: Break up their marriage on their wedding day?
sourceContext.nextLine: Who would do that?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 在他们婚礼当天把人家婚姻拆散？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Ross 对自己曾经的冲动念头进行道德审判时，break up a marriage 的分量非常重，不只是“让两人分手”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: break up 既能不及物分手，也能及物破坏一段关系。对象换成 marriage 后道德重量会更强。

sourceContext.previousLine: Tomorrow, Emily gets married again.
sourceContext.targetLine: So try to really keep Ross' mind off of it.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Okay. - Good idea.

definitions: 让 Ross 别老想着这事
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 所以尽量让 Ross 别一直想着这件事。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 朋友们知道 Ross 会越想越钻牛角尖，于是开始想办法转移注意力。这个结构在安慰和陪伴里非常实用。
zh-CN.usageNotes: keep my mind off it / take my mind off it 都很常见，表示转移注意力。

sourceContext.previousLine: Honey, it's okay. It's okay.
sourceContext.targetLine: How about I fix you a drink? Maybe a margarita?
sourceContext.nextLine: Ross has the blender.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 要不我给你调杯酒？来杯玛格丽塔怎么样？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 想安抚 Ross，于是搬出最美式的家庭社交动作之一: fix you a drink。
zh-CN.usageNotes: fix 在美式口语里能表示“给你准备点吃喝”。不是修理，而是动手张罗。

sourceContext.previousLine: Yeah.
sourceContext.targetLine: - Well, it's kind of dangerous. - Well, I like danger.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Okay, you guys free tonight? - Yeah.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 那是挺危险的。可我就喜欢刺激。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 对警察工作上头时，这句把她那种对冒险的迷恋说得特别直接。
zh-CN.usageNotes: I like danger / I like a challenge 都是很美式的自我塑造句，常用来表现胆大或嘴硬。

sourceContext.previousLine: Half the taste is in the smell. You're sucking up all the taste units.
sourceContext.targetLine: Okay, I'll give them back.
sourceContext.nextLine: What is so great about that sandwich?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 好吧，我会把它们还回去。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Phoebe 在“我只是借来玩玩”和“我得还回去”之间摇摆，give back 这种动作词很生活，但在剧情里经常是道德转折点。
zh-CN.usageNotes: give it back / give them back 口语极高频。反义常是 keep it。

term.text: the best sandwich in the world

sourceContext.previousLine: Okay, I got it.
sourceContext.targetLine: This place makes the best sandwich in the world.
sourceContext.nextLine: Does it have meatballs?

definitions: 全世界最好吃的三明治
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 这地方做的可是全世界最好吃的三明治。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Ross 的三明治 PTSD 还没过去，所以 sandwich 一下就成了情绪按钮。the best ... in the world 则是很典型的夸张口语。
zh-CN.usageNotes: the best X in the world 是极常见的主观夸法，朋友聊天里用起来完全自然。

source.episodeTitle: The One With The Ball

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One With The Ball

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E21 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

term.text: set up a little litter box

sourceContext.previousLine: I don't care what you think.
sourceContext.targetLine: I'm gonna go set up a little litter box for Mrs. Whiskerson.
sourceContext.nextLine: Well, what am I gonna call her? Fluffy?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我先去给 Mrs. Whiskerson 弄个小猫砂盆。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Rachel 收留猫后一下进入临时养宠模式，set up a litter box 就是非常具体的“先把生活配套搭起来”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: set up 在家居和照顾宠物场景里特别常见，带有“先布置好能用的东西”的意思。

sourceContext.previousLine: Uh-huh.
sourceContext.targetLine: I really don't want to mess up what we have.
sourceContext.nextLine: I'm just worried it's gonna be a big mistake.

definitions: 把我们现在这段关系搞坏
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我真的不想把我们现在拥有的这一切搞砸。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Gary 想更进一步时，Phoebe 最怕的是推进太快把现有关系毁掉。这个表达很适合学习“关系升级焦虑”的英语说法。
zh-CN.usageNotes: what we have 很常见，用来笼统指两人目前的关系状态，含糊但很真实。

sourceContext.previousLine: Oh, my... Oh, good God.
sourceContext.targetLine: I give up, you guys. I don't know what I'm gonna do with this thing.
sourceContext.nextLine: Baking it didn't help, huh?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我放弃了，伙计们。我真不知道拿这玩意怎么办。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Rachel 被猫折腾到彻底投降，give up 在这里是情绪认输，不是价值判断。
zh-CN.usageNotes: I give up! 很口语，适合表达被难题、设备、宠物、游戏折磨到不想继续。

term.text: find out what to do with it

sourceContext.previousLine: What am I gonna do, get a thousand regular cats?
sourceContext.targetLine: If you want, you can keep it until you find out what to do with it.
sourceContext.nextLine: No, Mon, that's not the point. I'm out a thousand dollars...

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你要愿意的话，可以先留着它，等你想清楚该怎么处理。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 朋友在给 Rachel 缓冲空间，不逼她立刻决定。find out what to do 很适合表达“先弄明白再说”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: what to do / what to say / where to go 这类疑问词+to do 结构在口语里超级高频。

sourceContext.previousLine: Well, so then what are you doing to me? Okay?
sourceContext.targetLine: Just get out of here, all right? Move on.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Jeez. - Wow, what an unusual cat.

definitions: 别停留在这儿了；赶紧往前
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 赶紧走吧，好吗？别在这儿耗着了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 说话人明显没耐心了，所以 move on 在这里更像驱赶而不是鼓励翻篇。
zh-CN.usageNotes: move on 既可温柔，也可很不耐烦。语气重时接近“别挡着了，走开”。

sourceContext.previousLine: Let's go, Team Monica.
sourceContext.targetLine: All right, we can work out the name later.
sourceContext.nextLine: Rachel, what is your cat doing in one of my bowls?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 行吧，名字之后再慢慢定。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Gary 一边推进同居，一边安抚“细节可以以后再说”。work out 在这里是“协商敲定”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: work out the details / the schedule / the name 都很常见，表示把细节谈妥。

sourceContext.previousLine: - So it looks like it's going well for you two. - I know. Really well.
sourceContext.targetLine: In fact, I'm gonna ask Phoebe to move in with me.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Oh, my God. - What do you think?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 其实，我准备邀请 Phoebe 搬来跟我一起住。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 在美国恋爱文化里，move in with someone 是关系升级的大节点，不只是“搬家”那么简单。
zh-CN.usageNotes: move in with someone 和 move in together 都很常见，通常隐含同居关系进入新阶段。

source.episodeTitle: The One With Joey's Big Break

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One With Joey's Big Break

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E22 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: Wow, um...
sourceContext.targetLine: Calm down?
sourceContext.nextLine: I'm trying, but, man, that guy can push my buttons.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 冷静点？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Joey 对“冷静点”这个建议本身就很不爽，所以反问式的 Calm down? 反而更能体现情绪。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 把 calm down 原样复述成问句，通常是在表示“你居然叫我冷静？”

sourceContext.previousLine: Yeah, um, I meet this girl in the subway and we fall in love in, like, a day, right?
sourceContext.targetLine: And then she disappears. But I find out where she lives.
sourceContext.nextLine: And when I get there, this old lady answers the door. I say, "Where's Betsy?"

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 然后她就消失了，可我还是查到了她住哪儿。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Joey 讲电影剧情时，find out where she lives 属于典型追查线索表达。
zh-CN.usageNotes: find out 后面接 wh- 从句特别高频，用来承接“调查到的信息内容”。

sourceContext.previousLine: ...because you actually have a very sweet little heinie.
sourceContext.targetLine: Man, I'm getting pretty tired. You might have to take over soon.
sourceContext.nextLine: We've been driving for a half-hour. And you haven't looked at the road once.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 哥们，我有点撑不住了，你可能得很快接手。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Monica 眼科诊疗现场太崩溃，只能叫 Rachel take over。这个词在轮班、交接、救场都很实用。
zh-CN.usageNotes: take over for me / take over soon 都很自然，意思是从我这里接过去继续。

sourceContext.previousLine: - What? - It's probably just temporary.
sourceContext.targetLine: We're hoping to get more money soon, so if you could just, uh, hang out.
sourceContext.nextLine: Hang out? How long?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我们希望很快能拿到更多钱，所以你能不能先在这儿等等。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 电影片场拖延发钱时，用 hang out 把“你先别走”说得像很轻松，实际上是在稳住人。
zh-CN.usageNotes: hang out 在这里不是社交，而是“先耗着、先别离开”。这是容易漏听的次高频义项。

term.text: a weird thing with my eye

sourceContext.previousLine: Ross, come on.
sourceContext.targetLine: All right, fine. I have a weird thing with my eye. Can we not talk about it please?
sourceContext.nextLine: All right.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 好吧，行。我眼睛这边有个很怪的问题，能不能别聊了？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Rachel 不想把病状说得太正式，于是用 a weird thing 这种含糊又自然的口语缓冲尴尬。
zh-CN.usageNotes: thing 在口语里经常用来模糊代称不想细讲的症状、问题或毛病。

sourceContext.previousLine: I just got off the phone with Estelle. Guess what?
sourceContext.targetLine: I got the lead in a movie.
sourceContext.nextLine: You got the lead in a movie? What's the movie about?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我拿到一部电影的男主角了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 对 Joey 来说，the lead 不只是角色，而是职业生涯终于上桌的证据。
zh-CN.usageNotes: get the lead / play the lead 都很常见。lead 在影视语境里读 /liːd/，不是 /lɛd/。

sourceContext.previousLine: It's not Richard, okay? It's this new guy. He's really good.
sourceContext.targetLine: I'm sorry. I'm not going to an eye doctor.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Oh, God. Here we go. - What?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 对不起，我就是不去看眼科医生。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Rachel 对看眼科极度抗拒，eye doctor 这种生活词在美剧里其实非常高频。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 正式一点可以说 ophthalmologist，但日常口语里几乎都说 eye doctor。

source.episodeTitle: The One In Vegas (1)

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One In Vegas (1)

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E23 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: Oh, you're so lucky!
sourceContext.targetLine: So where's Monica? Did you guys make up?
sourceContext.nextLine: - No. - But she just came up here.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 所以 Monica 呢？你们和好了吗？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Vegas 双集开头，大家最关心的就是 Chandler 和 Monica 有没有 make up。这个词在情侣吵架语境里高频到不能再高频。
zh-CN.usageNotes: make up after a fight / make up with someone 都自然。别和“编造”那个义项混掉。

sourceContext.previousLine: What the hell took you so long?
sourceContext.targetLine: You can't tell Chandler. I ran into Richard.
sourceContext.nextLine: - Which Richard? - The Richard.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你不能告诉 Chandler，我碰见 Richard 了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Monica 一见旧爱就心虚，run into someone 在这里就是那种完全没准备好的偶遇。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 比 meet 更强调偶然性。很常见于前任、熟人、尴尬对象突然出现的戏。

sourceContext.previousLine: Yeah.
sourceContext.targetLine: - Hey. - Don't come out here.
sourceContext.nextLine: I support you 100 percent and I wanna prove that to you in person.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 别出来，先别出来。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Monica 怕 Chandler 一出来就撞上 Richard，才会这么急着拦。短句很简单，但在剧情上是关键拦截动作。
zh-CN.usageNotes: come out here 带的是“从里面出来到我这边”。here 把空间关系说得很清楚。

sourceContext.previousLine: I've tried to apologize.
sourceContext.targetLine: If I can't do it on the phone, I'll go and do it in person.
sourceContext.nextLine: I heard that. Uh, can you put him on?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 如果电话里说不成，我就直接当面去说。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Ross 觉得某些事必须 face-to-face 解决，in person 把郑重感和压力都带出来了。
zh-CN.usageNotes: in person 很高频，适合谈见面聊、线下处理、本人出席。比 face-to-face 更中性。

sourceContext.previousLine: - Wanna talk to Chandler? - Let me talk to him.
sourceContext.targetLine: He didn't believe in my movie...
sourceContext.nextLine: ...which is a big mistake, because it is real. Real.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 他根本不相信我的电影……
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Joey 对项目的自尊心被 Richard 刺到，所以用 believe in my movie，不只是说“他不喜欢”，而是“他不信这事有前途”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: believe in 比 believe 更强，带价值认同、信心和支持感。

sourceContext.previousLine: That'll be nice to have the apartment to myself.
sourceContext.targetLine: To walk around naked.
sourceContext.nextLine: No, so I can be by myself. Have a little alone time.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 就那样光着到处走呗。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Vegas 酒店场景越来越放飞，walk around naked 用最直白的动作词把荒唐感拉满。
zh-CN.usageNotes: walk around + 形容词/状态 很常见，表示“以某种状态到处晃”。

source.episodeTitle: The One In Vegas (2)

deckMetadata.title: Friends · The One In Vegas (2)

summary.zh-CN: 基于 Friends S05E24 真实字幕台词重筛，优先保留中级学习者真正值得学的词、短语和口语结构。

sourceContext.previousLine: When you're playing a machine and it hasn't paid out...
sourceContext.targetLine: ...a lurker waits for you to give up and then...
sourceContext.nextLine: Kills you?

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: ……总会有个潜伏的人等着你放弃，然后……
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 赌场里讲机器和赌徒心理时，give up 指的不是人生放弃，而是“你一松手，机会就给了别人”。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 赌局、游戏、竞争里，give up 常带“你刚停下，别人就捡漏”的遗憾感。

sourceContext.previousLine: ...and we get married here, tonight.
sourceContext.targetLine: Shut up!
sourceContext.nextLine: It just got interesting!

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 闭嘴！
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Vegas 这集大家情绪都很满，shut up 常作为震惊、嫌弃、制止的爆点反应词。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 对朋友可作夸张惊叹，对陌生人则很冒犯。真实场合慎用。

sourceContext.previousLine: - What? - Everyone you lurk, I'll lurk first.
sourceContext.targetLine: You move on, I'll be one step ahead of you...
sourceContext.nextLine: ...every single time.

definitions: 你往前一步，我就先你一步
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 你继续往前走吧，我会永远比你快一步……
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 赌博狂热时，move on 不再是翻篇，而是继续推进游戏流程，带一点追逐感。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 同一个短语在不同场景里会换义，这正是美剧听力最值得学的地方。

sourceContext.previousLine: That's funny, I think I just did.
sourceContext.targetLine: - I have found my identical hand twin. - What's that?
sourceContext.nextLine: A guy with my identical hands. I'm gonna be a millionaire!

definitions: 手一模一样的双胞胎搭子
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我找到一个和我手长得一模一样的手部双胞胎了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Joey 进入 Vegas 后整个人都被奇怪执念带跑，identical hand twin 正是 Friends 式荒唐笑点。
zh-CN.usageNotes: 这是本集特有笑点词组，不是通用高频表达，但对理解剧情非常关键。

sourceContext.previousLine: Whoa, what kind of party was this?
sourceContext.targetLine: I've been walking around like this? You have so crossed a line.
sourceContext.nextLine: Rach, wait. The men's room is that way.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我之前一直是这样走来走去的？你这真的太越界了。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Monica 发现 Chandler 对自己做了过分的事后，直接用 crossed a line 下判断。这是关系边界表达里的核心口语。
zh-CN.usageNotes: cross a line 比 rude 更像“突破底线”。在亲密关系、职场和朋友冲突里都特别实用。

sourceContext.previousLine: Look, you and I have been given a gift.
sourceContext.targetLine: We have to do something with it. Like hand modeling. Or magic.
sourceContext.nextLine: And you know NASA's gonna want to talk to us.

zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 我们得拿这双手做点什么，比如去当手模，或者玩魔术。
zh-CN.sceneNotes: Joey 对“手部双胞胎”执念升级后，连 hand modeling 都认真考虑起来，特别符合 Friends 的无厘头推进。
zh-CN.usageNotes: modeling 不只指走秀，也能指局部模特工作，比如 hand modeling, print modeling。

term.text: what kind of party was this?

sourceContext.previousLine: Hey, you wet my pants!
sourceContext.targetLine: Whoa, what kind of party was this?
sourceContext.nextLine: I've been walking around like this? You have so crossed a line.

definitions: 这到底是什么离谱派对
zh-CN.exampleTranslation: 哇，这到底是个什么鬼派对？
zh-CN.sceneNotes: 醉酒后清醒回望现场时，what kind of ... is this? 是特别自然的震惊吐槽句型。
zh-CN.usageNotes: What kind of day is this? / What kind of place is this? 都很常见，适合表达荒谬感。
